'''Manari''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Manari system/Legends|Manari system]] of the [[Brevost sector/Legends|Brevost sector]],<ref name="EAOC" /> one of the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]]<ref name="Region" /> in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="EAOC" /> During the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Great Galactic War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]], Manari was ruled by tradition-bound leaders who had a very delicate [[Politics/Legends|political]] etiquette.<ref name="Crew Mission">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Diplomacy|Knife's Edge Politics}}</ref> In [[19 BBY/Legends|19.5 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'' states that the Battle of Manari took place two and a half years after the start of the Clone Wars, which according to the ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' began in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Republic forces fought in the [[Battle of Manari]] against an anti-Republic force. [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Roan Shryne]] lost [[Roan Shryne's second Padawan|his second Padawan]] during the battle and as a result, the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] became disillusioned about the war and lost hope in the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]].<ref name="DL">''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]''</ref> After the end of the war, Manari was part of the Republic's successor regime, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Manari was first mentioned in the [[November]] [[2005]] novel ''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]''. In [[2011]], Manari received another mention in the massively multiplayer online role-playing game ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''. The planet was mentioned in the Diplomacy crew skill mission "Knife's Edge Politics".
